checks.finding: All 13 requirements (REQ-001..REQ-013) are observable/measurable via concrete output fields and enumerated statuses. Each has at least one mapped executable AC: REQ-002 per-artifact status enum fully exercised by AC-002/003/004/005/017; REQ-005 spec_revision by AC-006/019; REQ-006 git_commit 40-hex shape by AC-007/008; REQ-008 no-key/no-sig-verify boundary by AC-011 + AC-014; REQ-009 host parity by AC-012; REQ-010 determinism by AC-013. No requirement is unverifiable intent.

Each Goal has a matching AC. Read-only deterministic re-verification -> AC-001 + AC-013. Exact parity with host canonical formulas -> golden AC-012. Maintain no-key-read/no-signature-verify boundary -> AC-011 + AC-014. No goal is left without observable coverage.

All 19 ACs describe externally observable output with a Test ID/type/target each. The three round-1 gap-closers are observable and non-vacuous: AC-017 asserts status invalid-recorded-sha with an explicit no-misclassify-to-mismatch guard and no-throw fail; AC-018 asserts empty-set canonical-digest comparison with vacuous-truth pass unless another invariant fails; AC-019 asserts spec_revision recompute to '' with match/mismatch branches and no throw. Each maps to REQs and a distinct TEST (017/018/019).

Non-goals are explicit and bound the plausible expansion surface: HMAC signature crypto verify (host-deferred), git_commit HEAD/ancestry verify (no-exec boundary, OQ-001/ADR-0008), bundle rewrite/repair/signature-generation, contract self re-verification, new network/subprocess/fs-write, identity-ledger involvement. Edge Cases and Security Boundaries reinforce the deferred-vs-in-process split.

Material constraints are stated: the central no-key-read/no-signature-verify control (REQ-008, B3), path-guard allowlist/denylist with allowlist-external -> path-denied (REQ-011), 2 MiB -> too-large, additive back-compatible v1 contract (REQ-012), determinism (REQ-010), verbatim reuse of host canonical formulas. Auth/authz delegation to OS-user boundary is explicit.

High-risk claims each carry a validation surface: canonical formula divergence -> golden parity AC-012; signature-key read leakage -> AC-011 + AC-014; exception on missing/oversize artifact -> exception-safety AC-004/005/017. No high-risk claim lacks a validation path, so SKIP does not apply.
